sections.paragraph:
συγχωρέω 1 aor. συνεχώρησα (LXX, Just.); pf. inf. συγκεχωρηκέναι (Just., D. 55, 1). Pass.: fut. 3 sg. συγχωρηθήσεται (Just., D. 77, 2); 1 aor. 3 sg. συνεχωρήθη (GrBar 1:1; 3:5); pf. συγκεχώρηται (Dg 8, 6; Just.); plpf. συγκεχώρητο (Just., D. 20, 1) (Trag., Hdt. et al.) prim. ‘to include by making room’.

① to display willingness to cooperate, grant a little ground τινί to someone (Thu. 1, 140, 5 al. in the sense of making some concession) IMg 3:1.

② to permit as a privilege, grant, permit τινί (to) someone (Bel 26; Jos., Ant. 3, 277) w. dat. and inf. (X., Cyr. 6, 3, 20; Diod S 38 + 39 Fgm. 8, 1; Appian, Bell. Civ. 5, 62 §260; Jos., Ant. 11, 6) mid. Ac 21:39 D.—Pass. (Herm. Wr. 1, 13a; cp. the pers. construction GrBar, s. above) pf. συγκεχώρηται it is granted Dg 8:6.—DELG s.v. χώρα. Sv.

aor. aor. = aorist

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

  1. pf. = perfect

inf. inf. = infinitive

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

fut. fut. = fut.

  1. sg. = singular

GrBar GrBar = Greek Baruch (Apocalypsis Baruchi Graece)—List 2

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

plpf. plpf. = pluperfect

Trag.

Trag. = writer (s) of tragedy

trag. = tragedy, writer (s) of tragedy

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

prim. prim. = primarily

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

IMg IMg = Ignatius to the Magnesians—List 1

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

dat. dat. = dative

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

pers. pers. = person(s)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Sv Sv (at the end of entries)=HSieben, Voces—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 954
